content.problemStatement:
There are 444 companies in the markets of Chefland, AAA, BBB, CCC, and DDD. \\
This year,


Company AAA made a profit of PPP lakh rupees,
Company BBB made a profit of QQQ lakh rupees,
Company CCC made a profit of RRR lakh rupees,
Company DDD made a profit of SSS lakh rupees.
There is said to be a **monopoly** in the market if the profit made by one company is **strictly greater than** the sum of profits made by all other companies. \\ Determine if there is a monopoly in the market or not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers PPP, QQQ, RRR and SSS — the profits made by companies AAA, BBB, CCC and DDD respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if there is a monopoly in the market. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers P, Q, R and S — the profits of companies AAA, BBB, CCC and DDD respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if any company's profit is strictly greater than the sum of the profits of the other three companies, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: DDD's profit 10 > 1+1+1=3 → YES.
Test case 2: No profit exceeds the sum of the other three → NO.
Test case 3: AAA's profit 100 > 90+3+4=97 → YES.
Test case 4: All profits are close; none is strictly greater than the sum of the others → NO.
The largest profit is 5, but 5 equals 2+2+1, not strictly greater, so there is no monopoly.
First case: 10 > 1+1+1=3 → YES. Second case: each profit is 3 and the sum of the other three is 9, so no monopoly → NO.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total sum of the four profits. For each company check if its profit is greater than totalSum - profit (i.e., the sum of the other three). If any check succeeds, answer is YES, otherwise NO. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
